dailyNews.title: US-Israel-Iran conflict impact goes beyond oil; European firms are paying through nose for chips — here's why
dailyNews.description: The Middle East conflict has disrupted air freight routes, cutting capacity and raising costs for semiconductor shipments from Asia to Europe. Companies are paying more or using inventories, while airlines carry less cargo due to longer routes and extra fuel needs.

dailyNews.description: The former counter-terrorism head resigned on Tuesday over the war with Iran, saying the country posed "no imminent threat" to the US.

dailyNews.description: Iran scheduled to play their three FIFA World Cup 2026 group games in United States, but Mexico is an alternative.

dailyNews.title: Snake venom case: Supreme Court quashes FIR, proceedings against YouTuber Elvish Yadav
dailyNews.description: The case against Elvish Yadav was registered on November 22, 2023, and he was arrested on March 17, 2024, for the alleged use of snake venom at a rave party in Noida, Uttar Pradesh.

dailyNews.title: Congress divided on Iran war with US-Israel? Manish Tewari, Shashi Tharoor back govt stance
dailyNews.description: Even as Rahul Gandhi slammed Narendra Modi-led government, his partymen - Tharoor and Tewari - support the centre's stand on the ongoing war in the middle east.

dailyNews.title: EXCLUSIVE: House Republicans to hold hearing on DHS shutdown risks amid travel surge
dailyNews.description: House Republicans scheduled a hearing on the impacts of the DHS shutdown as the funding standoff with Democrats continues.

dailyNews.description: Federal judge orders new transparency rules for grand jury proceedings after Trump's failed attempt to secure indictments against six Democratic lawmakers.

dailyNews.title: Iconic sports venue Madison Square Garden to host NYPD, staff at private concert amid 'Thank You' effort
dailyNews.description: The appreciation concert at Madison Square Garden exclusive to the NYPD is slated to feature high-profile performers. The iconic venue donated venue and production costs.

dailyNews.title: Miami (Ohio) gets help from raucous crowd on way to first March Madness win in 27 years
dailyNews.description: Miami (Ohio) defeated SMU, 89-79, in the First Four portion of the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament on Wednesday night and received the backing of their fans.

dailyNews.title: Uber expands women driver preference nationwide
dailyNews.description: Uber's Women Preferences feature lets female riders request female drivers nationwide for added safety and comfort during their rides, the company says.

dailyNews.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, was left to square the president’s comments about an imminent nuclear threat from Iran with a letter from one of her trusted aides.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says U.S. and Qatar Not Involved in Strike on Iran’s South Pars Gas Field
dailyNews.description: President Trump said Israel was responsible for the attack, but vowed to “massively” destroy the gas field if Tehran hit Qatar’s energy facilities in retaliation.

dailyNews.title: ‘Go Big and Go Loud’: Inside the Justice Dept.’s Push to Prosecute Protesters
dailyNews.description: Prosecutors have struggled to prove in court what the president and his aides have repeatedly said in public: that a network of leftist activists presents a serious threat to national security.

dailyNews.title: Trump Vowed to Crack Down on Fraudsters, but He’s Pardoned Dozens
dailyNews.description: Across both of his terms, President Trump has granted clemency to more than 70 allies, donors and others convicted in fraud cases.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Planning for America’s 250th Emphasizes Religion’s Role in the Nation’s Founding
dailyNews.description: A closed-door White House event included news about the National Garden of American Heroes and an emphasis on the role of religion in the founding.

dailyNews.title: How Japan’s Leader Sanae Takaichi Found Her Voice in D.C. Decades Ago
dailyNews.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi got her start in politics as an intern for a trailblazing Colorado lawmaker. She returns to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.

dailyNews.title: The Markets Contend with Powell’s Long Goodbye at the Fed
dailyNews.description: The chair of the Federal Reserve said that he might not leave the central bank any time soon, setting up a potential clash with President Trump.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to "blow up" huge Iranian gas field if Iran attacks Qatar
dailyNews.description: Trump says the U.S. will destroy a vast Iranian gas field if Tehran retaliates for an Israeli strike on the facility by attacking Qatar's infrastructure.

dailyNews.title: What to know about Joe Kent, Trump counterterrorism chief who resigned
dailyNews.description: In a resignation letter, Joe Kent said Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation," and he asserted that "we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby."

dailyNews.title: 2/27: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Jeffrey Epstein ties spark investigations and resignations; New York City Mayor Mamdani and President Trump talk housing in second White House meeting.

dailyNews.title: Iran Targets Energy Sites After Israel Hit World's Largest Gas Field
dailyNews.description: Iran attacked oil and gas sites in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait as the conflict rapidly becomes a war on the world’s energy supply. Iran says it is retaliation after Israel attacked the South Pars natural gas refinery on the Iranian coast which holds the largest gas reserves in the world. President Donald Trump says the United States was not involved in the original hit on the South Pars but is threatening to blow up the entirety of the gas field if Iran attacks Qat

dailyNews.title: FBI conducting leak investigation into former Trump official who resigned over Iran war
dailyNews.description: A source told NBC News that the probe began before Joe Kent, a longtime Trump ally, announced his resignation as director of the National Counterterrorism Center.

dailyNews.title: Tulsi Gabbard Declines to Say If Iran Posed an 'Imminent Threat'
dailyNews.description: With attacks on energy sites in the Middle East intensifying, President Donald Trump’s Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was pressed on Capitol Hill about whether the administration knew Iran would target oil and gas sites in retaliation for the war. She also deflected questions about whether Iran’s nuclear program posed an imminent threat to the United States. NBC’s Peter Alexander reports for TODAY from the White House.

dailyNews.title: Some DHS contractors told White House officials they were asked to pay Corey Lewandowski
dailyNews.description: Some companies complained to the Trump administration that Lewandowski has stood to personally profit from the DHS contracting process. Lewandowski denies the allegations.
